name: project_timeline_updates
description: Project Alpha launch date and budget undergo multiple revisions over several months.

actions.claim: Project Alpha is scheduled to launch in Q3 2026 with a budget of 2 million dollars.

evidence.content: Initial project plan approved by steering committee for Q3 2026 launch.

actions.claim: Project Alpha development team expanded from 8 to 12 engineers to meet Q3 2026 deadline.

evidence.content: Four additional engineers onboarded to accelerate development timeline.

actions.claim: Project Alpha launch has been postponed from Q3 2026 to Q4 2026 due to regulatory review requirements.

evidence.content: Regulatory compliance team identified additional review steps that require 3 extra months.

actions.claim: Project Alpha budget has been increased from 2 million to 3 million dollars to cover extended timeline and additional compliance costs.

evidence.content: CFO approved budget increase of 1 million dollars for regulatory compliance and extended development.

actions.claim: Project Alpha Q4 2026 launch confirmed with final budget of 3 million dollars after board approval.

evidence.content: Board of directors ratified revised timeline and budget at quarterly meeting.

questions.query: When is Project Alpha scheduled to launch?
What is the current budget for Project Alpha?
Why was the Project Alpha launch delayed?

name: team_changes_with_retraction
description: A false rumor about mass resignations is retracted, and the correct team size is established.

actions.claim: The engineering department currently has 20 full-time employees across three teams.

evidence.content: HR records show 20 FTEs in engineering as of Q1 headcount review.

actions.claim: Company-wide morale survey shows 85 percent satisfaction rate in engineering.

evidence.content: Anonymous survey completed by 18 of 20 engineers showed high satisfaction.

actions.claim: Five engineers have submitted resignation letters, reducing the engineering team from 20 to 15 members.

evidence.content: Unverified internal memo circulated claiming five resignations were submitted.

actions.claim: HR confirmed no resignations have been submitted; the engineering team remains at 20 full-time employees.

evidence.content: VP of HR issued official statement confirming all 20 engineers remain employed.

questions.query: How many engineers are on the engineering team?
Were there any resignations in the engineering department?
What is the morale level in engineering?

name: medical_diagnosis_revision
description: A patient initially diagnosed with Type 2 diabetes has the diagnosis revised to insulin resistance after additional testing.

actions.claim: Patient John Doe, age 52, presented with elevated fasting glucose of 140 mg/dL and HbA1c of 6.8 percent.

evidence.content: Lab results from annual physical showing elevated glucose markers.

actions.claim: Patient John Doe diagnosed with Type 2 diabetes based on fasting glucose and HbA1c levels.

evidence.content: Primary care physician documented Type 2 diabetes diagnosis in patient chart.

actions.claim: Endocrinologist ordered additional testing including oral glucose tolerance test and C-peptide levels for John Doe.

evidence.content: Specialist referral for comprehensive metabolic panel and insulin function testing.

actions.claim: John Doe diagnosis revised from Type 2 diabetes to insulin resistance based on normal C-peptide levels and OGTT results showing impaired glucose tolerance without diabetic threshold.

evidence.content: Endocrinologist revised diagnosis after C-peptide came back at 2.8 ng/mL (normal) and OGTT peaked at 185 mg/dL (below 200 threshold).

questions.query: What is John Doe's current diagnosis?
What tests led to the revised diagnosis for John Doe?
What were John Doe's initial glucose findings?

name: legal_contract_amendments
description: A service contract undergoes amendments to payment terms and duration after negotiation rounds.

actions.claim: Service contract between Acme Corp and Beta LLC signed with monthly payment of 50000 dollars and 12-month term starting January 2026.

evidence.content: Original contract executed with standard terms: $50,000/month for 12 months.

actions.claim: Acme Corp requested scope expansion to include data analytics services in addition to original consulting services.

evidence.content: Change request submitted by Acme Corp project manager for additional analytics deliverables.

actions.claim: Contract amendment 1 approved: monthly payment increased from 50000 to 65000 dollars to cover expanded analytics scope.

evidence.content: Amendment signed by both parties increasing monthly fee by $15,000 for analytics services.

actions.claim: Contract amendment 2 approved: term extended from 12 months to 18 months with the revised monthly payment of 65000 dollars.

evidence.content: Both parties agreed to 6-month extension at current rate to complete analytics integration.

questions.query: What is the current monthly payment in the Acme Corp contract?
How long is the current contract term between Acme Corp and Beta LLC?
Why was the contract payment increased?

name: scientific_hypothesis_revision
description: A pharmaceutical compound shows initially promising results that are significantly revised after Phase 2 trials.

actions.claim: Compound X showed 40 percent efficacy rate in Phase 1 clinical trials for treatment of chronic pain with no significant side effects observed.

evidence.content: Phase 1 trial with 50 participants showed 40% pain reduction vs placebo, no adverse events reported.

actions.claim: Research team published preliminary findings recommending Compound X for Phase 2 trials based on promising 40 percent efficacy.

evidence.content: Peer-reviewed publication in Journal of Clinical Pharmacology recommending advancement to Phase 2.

actions.claim: Phase 2 trials of Compound X with 500 participants showed revised efficacy of only 15 percent, significantly lower than the Phase 1 result of 40 percent.

evidence.content: Larger trial population revealed efficacy of 15% vs placebo, suggesting Phase 1 results were inflated by small sample size.

actions.claim: Phase 2 trials also revealed Compound X causes mild hepatotoxicity in 8 percent of participants, a side effect not observed in Phase 1.

evidence.content: Safety monitoring board flagged elevated liver enzymes in 40 of 500 participants (8%).

questions.query: What is the current efficacy rate of Compound X?
Were any side effects found for Compound X?
How did Phase 2 results compare to Phase 1 for Compound X?

name: budget_retraction_and_correction
description: Quarterly revenue figures are corrected after an audit reveals accounting errors in the initial report.

actions.claim: Q1 2026 revenue reported at 5.2 million dollars based on preliminary accounting figures.

evidence.content: Preliminary financial report showed Q1 revenue of $5.2M before audit review.

actions.claim: Q1 2026 operating expenses totaled 3.8 million dollars across all departments.

evidence.content: Expense report consolidated from all department budgets for Q1.

actions.claim: Internal audit found that Q1 2026 revenue was overstated due to double-counted receivables; corrected revenue is 4.7 million dollars.

evidence.content: Audit team identified $500K in duplicate invoice entries inflating Q1 revenue.

actions.claim: Official Q1 2026 revenue confirmed at 4.7 million dollars after audit correction of double-counted receivables.

evidence.content: CFO signed off on corrected Q1 financials showing $4.7M revenue.

questions.query: What is the Q1 2026 revenue?
Why was the Q1 revenue figure corrected?
What were the Q1 2026 operating expenses?

name: treatment_plan_evolution
description: A patient treatment plan evolves through multiple adjustments as new imaging reveals a different underlying condition.

actions.claim: Patient Sarah Chen prescribed physical therapy twice per week and ibuprofen 400mg for chronic lower back pain.

evidence.content: Orthopedist prescribed conservative treatment: PT 2x/week and NSAIDs for 6 weeks.

actions.claim: After 4 weeks, Sarah Chen reported minimal improvement; physical therapy increased to three times per week.

evidence.content: Follow-up visit showed VAS pain score unchanged at 7/10; PT frequency increased.

actions.claim: Sarah Chen switched from ibuprofen to acetaminophen 500mg due to gastrointestinal side effects from prolonged NSAID use.

evidence.content: Patient reported stomach upset and mild gastritis symptoms; medication changed to acetaminophen.

actions.claim: MRI scan revealed herniated disc at L4-L5 in Sarah Chen, explaining persistent pain despite conservative treatment.

evidence.content: MRI report showed moderate L4-L5 disc herniation with mild nerve root compression.

actions.claim: Sarah Chen referred to neurosurgeon for evaluation of L4-L5 herniated disc; physical therapy continued at three times per week with modified exercises.

evidence.content: Orthopedist referred patient to neurosurgery while continuing modified PT program.

questions.query: How often does Sarah Chen attend physical therapy?
What pain medication is Sarah Chen currently taking?
What did the MRI reveal for Sarah Chen?
Why was Sarah Chen referred to a neurosurgeon?

name: clause_retraction_legal
description: A non-compete clause is removed from employment contracts after a change in state law renders it unenforceable.

actions.claim: Standard employment contract at TechCo includes a 2-year non-compete clause covering all competitors within 50-mile radius.

evidence.content: Employment agreement template section 8.3 specifies 2-year non-compete with geographic restriction.

actions.claim: TechCo has 150 employees currently bound by the 2-year non-compete clause in their employment agreements.

evidence.content: HR records show 150 active employees signed agreements containing non-compete provisions.

actions.claim: State legislature passed Senate Bill 1247 effective March 1 2026, prohibiting non-compete clauses for employees earning below 150000 dollars annually.

evidence.content: New state law invalidates non-compete agreements for employees below $150K salary threshold.

actions.claim: TechCo non-compete clause removed from all employment contracts effective March 2026 following Senate Bill 1247; affected 120 of 150 employees earning below the 150000 dollar threshold.

evidence.content: Legal department issued contract amendments removing non-compete for employees below salary threshold.

questions.query: Does TechCo still enforce a non-compete clause?
What law changed the non-compete situation at TechCo?
How many TechCo employees were affected by the non-compete removal?
